_extracted.professional_summary: Experienced professional with commitment and academic grounding in international development; and expertise in Women, Peace, and Security (WPS) at multiple levels - from grassroots implementation to research and publishing. Experience in leading high-impact projects that advance sustainable development and empower marginalised communities, in collaboration with international organisations. focusing on critical areas such as gender equality and expanding economic and educational opportunities for minorities.

employment.description_of_duties: Directed the organisation's efforts to combat gender-based violence in the Afar region of Ethiopia through programmes that promote girls' enrolment in education and post-secondary pathways, as well as initiatives advancing women's financial inclusion by establishing a women's cooperatives. In the challenging context of the civil war of Ethiopia, I pivoted towards addressing the gendered impacts of conflict and led war emergency response efforts including the delivery of essential medical equipment to those heavily affected by war.

Founded Lem Chaka, a company that promotes circular economy through the recycling of food waste sourced from hotels in Addis Ababa to produce high-quality organic fertilizers. Led a team of 12 people to establish the company from the ground up. Ensured that each initiative went beyond environmental responsibility; and tackled extreme poverty and the inclusion of the most vulnerable groups in every project. Recipient of a $246,000 grant from USAID for the implementation of CapEx equipments.

employment.employer: Office of the President of Ethiopia
employment.description_of_duties: Collaborated with and reported to H.E. Sahle-Work Zewde, during her tenure as President of Ethiopia, to provide strategic guidance and develop key policy recommendation on gender equality during high-level international conferences. Conferences attended: Global Women Leaders High-Level Dialogue (Madrid, January 2024), High-Level Dialogue on Women in Conflicts in the Great Lake Region (Luanda, October 2024), High-Level Forum on the Future of Africa-Europe Relations (Brussels, November 2024).

employment.employer: African Women Peace and Security Institute
employment.description_of_duties: Gender consultant at the African Women Peace and Security Institute (AWPSI). I fulfilled various roles including research, coordination, and logistical support to build the AWPSI. Led research totalling over 140 pages involving a comparative analysis on the implementation of peace agreements, some spearheaded by women, in conflict-affected nations. Analysed and drew insights from the gendered impacts of conflicts in Afghanistan, Colombia, Liberia, and the DRC resulting in a roadmap proposal for post-conflict Ethiopia.

Consultant at the United Nations Economic Commission for Africa in the Gender, Poverty, and Social Policy (GPSP) Division, focusing on extreme poverty and gender equality. Supported the Chief of the GPSP division in the drafting and preparation of meetings, external presentations and technical proposals.

Consultant at the Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights in the Eastern Regional Office (OHCHR-EARO). Co-headed missions related to Djibouti. Fostered collaborations with Civil Society Organisations (CSOs) in Djibouti, engaged in exchanges to empower them, conducted trainings and collectively addressed the challenges they encountered. Contributed to the communications team responsible for weekly reporting to headquarters in Geneva.

_extracted.professional_summary: As a Digital Financial Services (DFS) IT Manager with extensive experience in digital transformation and IT infrastructure, I drive the delivery of secure, efficient, and customer-centric digital banking solutions. With a background spanning system engineering, systems administration, and solutions architecture at leading fintech organizations in Ethiopia, I combine technical proficiency with managerial expertise and industry knowledge. My multifaceted skill set encompasses IT infrastructure design, digital financial services operations, project management, leadership, complex problem solving, and cybersecurity risk management. I am committed to leveraging fintech innovations and industry best practices to enhance digital service offerings while ensuring regulatory compliance and business continuity.

employment.description_of_duties: Develop and implement digital financial products and services, ensuring alignment with business objectives and customer needs. Stay abreast of fintech trends and innovations, integrating new technologies to enhance digital financial services offerings. Ensure regulatory compliance and adherence to financial industry standards in all digital financial services operations. Lead strategic planning efforts for digital financial services, including market analysis, product design, and rollout strategies. Manage partnerships with fintech companies, mobile money operators, financial institutions, and stakeholders to expand digital service offerings. Oversee risk management processes to identify and mitigate cybersecurity threats, fraud risks, and data security issues. Provide leadership to a team of professionals, offering guidance, mentorship, and support for achieving organizational goals. Execute project management methodologies to ensure the successful implementation of digital financial service projects.

A solutions architect that requires extensive knowledge of IT infrastructure. My responsibility is to develop solutions for an organization. I design and implement IT solutions for improving an organization's operations. My role is to evaluate business needs and develop solutions to solve issues in the company. I provide recommendations to the company and stakeholders on what type of solutions would maximize their productivity or reduce issues in their operations. And also collect relevant documentation to develop technical roadmaps and propose solutions. The senior system administrator oversees the systems administration department within an organization. My responsibilities include supervising our Operations team, monitoring system performance and configuring new software and hardware. To be successful in this role, I have excellent troubleshooting skills and hands-on experience with various operating systems. Manage systems administrators who oversee day-to-day problems. This is a managerial role that requires leadership skills and demonstrated experience in systems administration. Perform cost-benefit and return-on-investment analyses for proposed systems to aid management in making implementation decisions. Interact and negotiate with vendors, outsourcers, and contractors to secure system-related products and services. Developing and implementing backup and recovery strategies to protect data and ensure business continuity.

System engineer Overseeing the development, installation, configuration, and administration of computing systems. Troubleshooting technical issues and developing effective solutions. Keeping abreast of the latest advancements in technology, protocols, and best practices. Apply proven communication, analytical, and problem-solving skills to help identify, communicate, and resolve issues to maximize the benefit of IT systems in the organization. My role is to ensure the stability, integrity, and efficient operation of the in-house information systems that support core organizational functions. Developing and implementing backup and recovery strategies to protect data and ensure business continuity.

employment.employer: France Development Agency-AFD (Seconded at Ethiopia's Ministry of Planning and Development)
employment.description_of_duties: Led the preparation of macroeconomic and sectoral reforms, influencing policy decisions. Supported the preparation of the Voluntary National Report (VNR) on Ethiopia's progress towards Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), enhancing international recognition of Ethiopia's efforts. Contributed to the preparation Ethiopia's Midterm Investment and Development Plan (2023/24-2025/26), and Homegrown Economic Reform II, the second generation of Economic Reform which the Ethiopian government is currently implementing. Conducted various economic analysis including the impact of the Ukraine-Russia war on Ethiopia, food balance estimation for wheat and maize 2022/23 fiscal year, the impact of electric tariff revision on poverty, Economic Impact of lifting fuel subsidy, providing actionable insights for policy adjustments. Advised MoPD leadership during engagements with International Financial Institutions (IMF, WB), securing financial and technical support for national projects. Provided capacity building training for technical staff on data analytics and visualization using R Software, improving analytical capabilities within the ministry.

Developed technical proposals for various development projects (12+ technical proposals on various development related issues). Led and coordinated 3 World Bank-funded projects and 1 project for GIZ with a total project budget of $560,000 USD. Developed technical reports & project documents. Provided weekly updates to clients on progress of projects that I manage.

Developed and implemented sustainability strategies and enhanced resource efficiency for various clients. Collaborated with multiple stakeholders, including government agencies and private sector partners, to promote sustainable development initiatives.

Provided technical assistance and guidance for the implementation of GEF projects, ensuring alignment with global environmental goals.

employment.employer: Addis Ababa University, Department of Economics
employment.description_of_duties: Taught Macroeconomics & Monetary Economic to undergraduate students. Prepared lectures and exercises, administered and graded examinations.

employment.employer: Debre Birhan University, Department of Economics
employment.description_of_duties: Taught various economics courses to undergraduate students (Microeconomics, Macroeconomics, Environmental and Natural Resource Economics, Econometrics). Prepared teaching modules, advised final year students.

_extracted.key_qualifications: Economic Policy Analysis; Reform Implementation; Climate Finance Mechanisms; Sustainability Analysis; Project Management; Data Analysis using R and Microsoft Office Suite; Macroeconomic Analysis; Sectoral Reforms; Voluntary National Report (VNR) preparation; Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) alignment; Midterm Investment and Development Plan preparation; Economic Reform implementation; Impact analysis (trade wars, tariff policy, subsidy reform); International Financial Institutions engagement (IMF, World Bank); Capacity building and training delivery; Technical proposal development; World Bank and GIZ project management; Data collection tool development (questionnaires, FGD guides, KIIs); Technical report writing; Sustainability strategies development; Resource efficiency optimization; Stakeholder collaboration and engagement; Export promotion and trade analysis; Live animal and meat export studies; Teff export feasibility analysis; Export performance evaluation; International benchmarking; National Export Plan development; COVID-19 response strategy; Environmental project proposal development; GEF project implementation support; Desk research; Environmental finance mechanisms; Teaching and curriculum development (Microeconomics, Macroeconomics, Environmental and Natural Resource Economics, Econometrics, Monetary Economics); Student mentoring; Strong Analytical and Problem-Solving Skills; Excellent Communication and Interpersonal Skills; Stakeholder Engagement; Advanced Data Analytics and Visualization; Climate Risk and Finance mechanisms knowledge; Sustainable Development understanding.
certifications: Advanced Data Analytics and Visualization using R Software
publications: Tsadkan, A. (2014). The Nexus between Public Spending and Economic Growth in Ethiopia: Lambert Publisher, Berlin, Germany.

_extracted.professional_summary: Yesu Girma Taye is a seasoned finance professional with a distinguished career spanning the banking, digital financial services, microfinance, and agribusiness sectors. Known for his sharp analytical skills and strategic problem-solving abilities, Yesu has a proven track record of delivering tailored financial solutions that drive organizational growth and long-term sustainability. His expertise lies in blending technical finance acumen with business leadership to tackle complex challenges, enabling organizations to thrive in dynamic and often volatile markets. Over the course of his career, Yesu has successfully led high-impact investments and conducted in-depth feasibility studies across multiple industries, including agribusiness, construction, and financial services. His leadership has been instrumental in guiding organizations toward financial stability, operational efficiency, and scalable growth, particularly through innovative financing solutions. One of Yesu's core strengths is his ability to manage projects that demand a deep understanding of regulatory environments, complex financial modeling, and stakeholder coordination. His approach to problem-solving is holistic—he considers financial, operational, and strategic factors to craft solutions that are both pragmatic and forward-looking. Whether establishing new microfinance institutions, securing capital for large-scale investments, or preparing businesses for investment readiness, Yesu consistently delivers measurable results. His ability to work seamlessly with international teams across diverse cultural contexts has further solidified his reputation as a global leader in financial consulting.

employment.description_of_duties: As the Project Team Leader for the Ethiopia Connect Project at AMAS, I play a pivotal role in driving strategic initiatives to enhance financial inclusion, particularly access to finance for smallholder farmers in Ethiopia. I am leading the establishment of a digital-led microfinance institution (MFI) in collaboration with both national and international consortium parties of my project by maintaining respect for cultural and individual diversities. My leadership was instrumental in raising 200% of the required capital to launch the MFI. Additionally, I manage stakeholder relationships, represent the organization in key meetings and oversee multiple high-value financial models and feasibility studies.

At Resilience BV, I spearheaded an assignment within the framework of the Ethio-Netherlands Seed Partnership project. My primary responsibility was to develop an in-depth financing strategy tailored specifically for seed-producing companies. This role entailed a thorough assessment of the financial needs and challenges faced by these companies, and devising a strategy that would enable them to access essential financial services. My approach involved identifying funding sources that would support the growth and scaling of seedling operations. By ensuring that these companies had the financial backing needed, I contributed in fostering their development and success within the agricultural sector.

As a Freelance Consultant at Precise Consult, I successfully led a significant assignment commissioned by the International Finance Corporation, which was centered around the development of an oilseed sourcing and financing strategy. I analyzed market trends, financial flows, and sourcing mechanisms to craft a comprehensive strategy that not only met the project's objectives but also provided actionable insights and recommendations. My contributions were instrumental in shaping a strategy that facilitated efficient sourcing and financing processes, thereby supporting the broader goals of the IFC's initiative. As a result, IFC can design a financial product that benefits more than 150,000 smallholder farmers.

As a Senior Agri-Finance Specialist at the Ethiopian Agricultural Transformation Agency (ATA), I led the design and implementation of innovative financial schemes to support financial institutions and Agricultural businesses. I spearheaded partnerships with different financial institutions to facilitate asset leasing, wholesale financing, and SME credit lines. My role involved managing grants, preparing budgets, and delivering comprehensive reports to stakeholders. I provided technical assistance to 35 agribusiness companies, helping them achieve investment readiness through financial management, business planning, and ESG compliance. My leadership and communication skills were key in building strong partnerships and ensuring the success of these initiatives.

employment.employer: United Bank S.C (Now, Hibret Bank S.C)
employment.position: Various Roles up to Credit Analyst
employment.description_of_duties: At United Bank S.C., I progressed through various roles, ultimately serving as a Credit Analyst. In this capacity, I appraised over $10.3 million in working capital loans and financed projects worth more than $7 million by conducting thorough feasibility studies. My responsibilities included conducting market research, financial analysis, due diligence, and monitoring market trends to support potential transactions. I managed a credit portfolio of $4.67 million and handled essential trade finance documents like Letters of Credit, Cash Against Documents, and Telegraphic Transfers. I consistently demonstrated strong analytical, communication, and decision-making skills in this dynamic banking environment.

_extracted.key_qualifications: Financial modeling and valuation analysis; feasibility study design and execution; financial analysis and risk analysis; credit appraisal and portfolio management; business due diligence and transaction management; project finance and deal structuring; microfinance institution establishment and scaling; agricultural finance and agribusiness financing; digital financial services strategy and implementation; asset leasing and wholesale financing structures; SME credit line development; investment readiness assessment and business planning; ESG compliance and sustainability integration; stakeholder relationship management and coordination; regulatory environment navigation and compliance; capital raising and fundraising; market research and trend analysis; financial modeling for various industries including agriculture, construction, manufacturing, and financial services; trade finance documentation and management (Letters of Credit, Cash Against Documents, Telegraphic Transfers); grant management and budget preparation; report writing and communication; multicultural team leadership; business plan development; financial product design; sourcing strategy development; oilseed sourcing and financing mechanisms; seed company financing strategy; supply chain finance; international investor negotiation and management; commercial bank relationship management and negotiation; financial institution partnership development; strategic problem-solving and holistic financial planning; working capital loan appraisal; project financing management; organizational financial stability and efficiency; revenue stream identification and unlocking; organizational resilience building.
certifications: Agricultural and Rural Finance Program (FAR 2023), FAO and ADA
Financial Modeling and Valuation Analyst (FMVA), Corporate Finance Institute, Jun 2023
Various Leadership Courses - Percipio Jan 2022 – Dec 2022
Financial Globalization, Capital Flows, and the Global Financial Cycle, ADIB December 2020
International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS), Addis Ababa University, School of Commerce August 2019
Domestic Banking, Credit Banking, International Banking Operations, United Bank S.C October 2014 – March 2015
publications: Taye, Yesu G. "Determinants of Job Satisfaction in Ethiopian Banking Industry: A Survey on Employees of Private Commercial Banks in Addis Ababa City." Saarbrücken: LAP Lambert Academic Publishing, 2019.

_extracted.professional_summary: Monitoring and Evaluation specialist with 17+ years of professional experience in development aid, humanitarian relief, and management consulting. Strong track record in designing, implementing, and managing comprehensive Monitoring, Evaluation and Learning (MEL) systems, with over 10 years of specialized experience coordinating M&E systems in Ethiopia and the East Africa region. Proven expertise in leading M&E components of large donor-funded programmes (USAID, DFID, EU), managing teams of monitoring and evaluation officers across multiple countries, and developing impact assessment and knowledge management frameworks. Excellent leadership skills in managing programmes and projects of diverse donors, with demonstrated ability to build effective networks with local and international stakeholders and high-level government officials. Strong communication and presentation capabilities with proven experience in preparing comprehensive reports and organizing stakeholder review and lesson-learning events.

education.degree: Master's in International Development Policy & Politics
Master's in Public Management and Policy

employment.description_of_duties: Effective leadership to design, implement and quality assure complexity-aware mixed methods M&E systems that provide information that meets the needs for relevant stakeholders and informs decision-making. Provide technical leadership to develop team members' performance monitoring, evaluation, and learning capacity to deliver robust M&E systems. Ensuring effective working relationships within teams and with clients and ongoing buy into M&E. HR: Identifying recruitment gaps and working with HR and the Practice Manager to recruit and train appropriate candidates. Design, develop, and implement a comprehensive and standardised Monitoring and Evaluation SOP tailored to the needs of the Genesis Health Practice. Based on the approved M&E Plan/Results Framework, support team members to implement data management systems to produce high-quality data that enable routine performance monitoring through being responsible for. Providing technical oversight, guidance and management of the UNAIDS Technical support Mechanism for the global HIV AIDS program M&E team. Serve as M&E Focal Point to lead on assigned TSM assignments and prepare, or contribute to, key assignment-related deliverables and required TSM MEL outputs/reports, including completing technical reviews on funding requests and other key TSM deliverables. Lead target setting for the annual scope of work statement.

Business Environment Investment Climate Project (6MEuro, EU funded) - Objective: To contribute to the improvement of the business environment in Ethiopia, to foster private sector development and the generation of employment and income opportunities in the context of the EODB programme of the government. Manage the day to day activities of the performance-based assessment framework for the project, including planning, coordination of inputs, ensuring that outputs are delivered and that the overall quality of the services provided is maintained. Other tasks include the following: Monitor project budget & resource present for management decisions. Quality check Consultant studies for the different thematic areas. Oversee communication activities, reports from consultants and lead report writing. Set up the project M& E plan and develop tools and templates as needed for effective operation of the monitoring system. Analysis of data and evidences and presenting for learning and decision making. Manage the Logframe of the program, revise the indicators and report progress on their achievement. Support the development of communications and visibility plan and implementation of the plan. Collect and process data and information from beneficiaries for monitoring of the program purpose. Support the team leader in coordinating with the key stakeholders and networks. Support the stakeholder engagement and relationship managment. Support the team leader in coordination and communication with clients & project partners. Support the team leader in completion of all project components within the agreed time frames and achievement of defined indicators. Liaise with key stakeholders to assess impact and learnings. Monitor the risks of the program using risk register tool. Ensure project implementation according to the ToR. Manage project evaluation.

Ethiopian Investment Advisory Facility (GBP 40M, DFID/FCDO-funded) - Objective: provide flexible, demand-driven technical assistance to Government of Ethiopia (GoE) ministries, agencies, state-owned enterprises and industrial parks in order to enhance the effectiveness of public investments and improve the business enabling environment, particularly for exporters. Led the monitoring & evaluation aspect of the Ethiopia Investment Advisory Facility, monitoring project outputs, aligning project outputs with programme objectives. Worked with project leads and a team of consultants to set Key Performance Indicators (KPI) to assess the impact of the overall programme and designed M&E frameworks for each project. Reviewed logframe indicators and reported against each impact, outcome and output indicators. Quality assurance of project proposals, write up of reports. Liaised with DFID and GoE (Ministry of Finance, Ministry of Revenue, Ethiopia Investment Commission, Industrial Parks Development Corporation, Ministry of Transport) on a regular basis to align on measuring impact and gathering lessons learned. Developed tools to assess the impact of capacity building measures for ministry and investment commission staff. Developed KPI dashboards and coached project teams to deliver agreed KPIs. Collated, collected and analysed monitoring data and reported to senior management team to update strategies and directions as needed. Supported project managers in regular monitoring, report writing, communicating impact and lessons learned. Developed MEL strategy, logframe & value for money strategy & risk matrix for the programme. Set programme meeting agendas and facilitate review meetings, lessons learned meetings. Managed online database and knowledge management system of the programme, ensuring the documentation of all project outputs in the online library. Supported the development of research on gender ''Power of parity'' reports in Ethiopia and worked with other organizations to disseminate the findings of the study.

Disasters and Emergencies Preparedness Programme (DEPP) (GBP 40M, DFID-funded). Developed the M&E, documentation and knowledge management systems of the programme in focus countries and managed the learning and M&E components of 14 projects in 10 countries in Africa, Asia and the Middle East. Developed proposals on early warning systems and public health emergency preparedness programmes and consulted more than 14 project managers in setting up M&E system and indicators to track impact. Developed a range of innovative and traditional approaches of monitoring and evaluation to support the programme in maximizing uptake and impact of programmatic evidence. Trained partners and international NGO staff on M&E and learning and managed the team of learning advisers in focus countries. Analysis of M&E data. Developed ToRs for field research, prepared case studies, blogs and articles. Conducted surveys, assessments and baselines in DRC, Kenya and Ethiopia to inform the design of preparedness programmes and to track impact of the different projects. Developed communication strategy, marketing strategy of the project website. Engaged with different stakeholders to communicate programme impact. Engaged on social media to communicate learnings (Twitter, Facebook, etc). Set up an agenda for different regional and international conferences in the area of preparedness and capacity building. Facilitated a number of high-level international and regional conferences and panel discussions on localisation, capacity building and other major humanitarian issues. Worked to facilitate collaboration in the sector between governments, bilateral, multilateral and local organizations in each country to leverage impact and sustain the impact of the programme. Engaged with donors, consultancy firms and contracting agencies.

employment.employer: Christian Aid UK, Ethiopia Programme Office
employment.description_of_duties: Successfully established an M&E framework for the country programme by closely working with the country director and programme managers in identifying how each project contributes to the overall programme objective. The framework was used by other similar Christian Aid programmes to monitor the impact of projects. Defined indicators that are measurable, monitored the projects with reference to the agreed indicators. Led the monitoring and evaluation of all projects in the areas of health, livelihood, climate change and humanitarian relief, coordinating with the respective programme officers. Trained national M&E staff on monitoring and evaluation, accountability and knowledge management. Documented and shared best practices and learnings with headquarters, different country teams and civil society through publications, blog posts, news updates, articles on the project website. Led international community of practice on monitoring and evaluation, learning and accountability on online meetings. Developed downward accountability framework for the country programme and ensured roll-out of necessary tools and systems to partner organizations. Prepared annual reports and case studies to the international programme representing the country programme. Worked on the design and implementation of the DFID-funded Building Resilience and Adaptation to Climate Extremes and Strategic climate institutions programmes and contributed to the design of an M&E framework and lesson-learning strategy. Conducted different case studies to measure impacts of climate change in rural Ethiopia and designed climate adaptation strategies and livelihood options.

Back to Work Livelihood Programme USAID Funded Program (Food By Prescription). Led and coordinated the implementation of the programme including programme quality assurance; technical support to implementing partners. Advocacy and collaboration with government, private sector and other international organizations dedicated to employment creation, built partnerships to coordinate livelihood strategies.

Developed capacity building strategy for Save the Children's Economic Strengthening Programme and provided coaching support to partners. Worked on quality assurance on proposals for effective programme implementation. Managed the operations of the programme with the implementing partners. Deployed monitoring tools for training programmes and livelihood projects. Assisted in the documentation and dissemination of best practices and lessons learned on the implementation of economic strengthening interventions among the various stakeholders. Conducted trainings for implementing partners and community group on sustaining impact and livelihoods. Preparation of annual work plan, programme documents and reports for the programme.

World Vision Areal Development Programme – multi-sector programme (GBP 500,000, institutional funding). Participated in programme design, redesign and logframe development. Worked in the rural area with the community on youth livelihood opportunities and value chain access for farmers to create income opportunities. Experience in value chain programme in eastern Oromia in working closely with the government cooperative office and private farms. Networked with micro financing institutions (MFIs), Ministry of Finance and Economic Cooperation and district/woreda management to improve farmers' access to credit facilities. Worked on sponsorship programmes for children to ensure access to basic services like education, health, water, in the rural area. Worked in emergency response and humanitarian programmes. Awarded by the regional government for successfully establishing a number of income generating activities for youth.
Prepared and taught business and economics classes for grades 11 and 12.

_extracted.key_qualifications: 17+ years of professional experience in development aid, humanitarian relief, and management consulting. 10+ years of specialist experience coordinating Monitoring, Evaluation and Learning (MEL) systems in Ethiopia and the East Africa region. Excellent leadership skills in managing programmes and projects of diverse donors (USAID, DFID, EU). Strong track record in creating effective networks with local and international project stakeholders and developing solid working relationships with high-level officials, including Ministry of Finance and Economic Cooperation, Ministry of Innovation and Technology, Ministry of Trade and Regional Integration, Ministry of Revenue, Ethiopia Investment Commission, Industrial Parks Development Corporation, Ministry of Transport, and National Disaster and Risk Management Commission. 10+ years of experience in designing and implementing M&E frameworks and tools including development of holistic Monitoring, Evaluating and Learning, logframe and value for money strategies for donor-funded demand-driven technical assistance programmes, implementation of impact assessment and knowledge management tools for multi-country research and case studies, and design of country-level M&E frameworks for multiple projects. 10+ years of experience leading M&E components of various projects including the Ethiopian Investment Advisory Facility (focused on improving business enabling environment), Disasters & Emergencies Preparedness Plan, and Business Environment Investment Climate Project. 6+ years of experience in development aid and humanitarian projects focused on economic strengthening, value chain development, access to market for MSMEs, and private sector engagement for job creation. Proven experience in leading and coordinating teams of monitoring and evaluation officers in Ethiopia and the region (Kenya, DRC, and Jordan), including monitoring performance, providing high-level guidance, training and on-the-job technical support, and advising on stakeholder management. Strong communication and presentation skills with proven experience in preparing and presenting annual reports, monthly reports, project closure reports, and organizing quarterly review meetings and lesson-learning events. Familiar with Digital M&E systems including OPSYS (EU Program management system), Kobo, SPSS, and other monitoring tools. Fully computer literate, proficient user of Microsoft Office. Experience in managing M&E activities, strategy, logframe, knowledge management and reporting in large donor-funded facilities. Expertise in designing and implementing performance-based assessment frameworks, quality assurance of project proposals, logframe management and indicator revision, risk management using risk register tools, knowledge management system and online database management, KPI dashboard development, and coaching of project teams. Experience in conducting surveys, assessments, baselines, impact assessments, and case studies. Skills in documentation and dissemination of best practices and lessons learned. Experience in developing capacity building strategies and coaching support to partners. Proficiency in value chain programme development and implementation. Experience in facilitating collaboration between governments, bilateral, multilateral, and local organizations. Skills in social media engagement and communication strategy development.
certifications: Chevening Scholar
publications: 2015 Asnake, H., 'Preparing for Drought: Reflections from Ethiopia'
2016 Asnake, H., 'Sharing Lessons about Crisis Preparedness in Ethiopia'
2016 Asnake, H., 'A Journey Towards Localisation of Aid'
